nativeRange.habitat: Coral reefs and lagoons at 1–30 m depth, typically associated with host sea anemones on outer reefs and reef slopes.
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical Red Sea / Gulf of Aden coral reef with host anemone (Entacmaea quadricolor, Heteractis crispa, Heteractis magnifica).

diet.notes: Generally accepts a wide range of meaty foods (mysis, brine shrimp, chopped seafood) and herbivore preparations (spirulina flake/pellet). A varied diet with both animal protein and marine algae is commonly advised.

summary: Designer captive-bred color morph of the Red Sea Two-Band Clownfish (Amphiprion bicinctus) showing irregular white spots or blobs between the two body bands; semi-aggressive and reef-safe.
careNotes: The Spotcinctus is a designer color morph of Amphiprion bicinctus, first produced by ORA, distinguished by irregular white spots or 'blobs' between the species' two standard white body bands; some individuals show blue-tinted 'Pearl Eyes'. Care is essentially the same as the parent species. Generally considered hardy once established and forgiving of minor parameter swings within typical reef ranges, but most retail sources flag it as more aggressive than Ocellaris/Percula, so it is commonly recommended to introduce it late and to avoid mixing with other clownfish species. A host anemone (Entacmaea quadricolor, Heteractis crispa, or Heteractis magnifica) is appreciated but not required; the fish often adopts corals, rockwork, or even powerheads in lieu of one. Tank-size guidance varies widely across sources (LiveAquaria 30 US gal, Reeflings 100 L, ReefApp 200 L); given the species' territorial behaviour and tendency to defend a sizable area around its host, a 150 L (≈40 US gal) tank with a long footprint is a sensible minimum. Clownfish are protandrous (male-first) sequential hermaphrodites — in a pair, the larger individual matures into a female; if she is removed the surviving male transitions over weeks to months. Care level is given as intermediate to reflect the spread between 'easy/hardy' (Reeflings, ReefApp) and 'difficult' (LiveAquaria); first-time saltwater keepers should be aware of the semi-aggressive temperament and host-defense behaviour.
breedingNotes: Has been reared in captivity for decades; ORA produces the Spotcinctus morph itself from captive broodstock. Forms monogamous breeding pairs that lay adhesive demersal eggs on a flat surface adjacent to the host anemone or rockwork; the male tends and aerates the clutch. Eggs typically hatch in roughly 6–11 days and larvae require live rotifers followed by enriched baby brine shrimp in a dedicated larval-rearing tank. Designer morph traits do not always pass cleanly to offspring, so commercial Spotcinctus production tends to involve line-breeding.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary clownfish collection root used per Stage A manifest. The Spotcinctus morph itself is not currently listed at LiveAquaria, so the parent-species product page (Two Banded Clownfish, product/3459) was used for husbandry parameters; the morph-specific information was taken from ORA (secondary).

additional.name: orafarm.com
additional.notes: Originator of the Spotcinctus designer morph; confirmed scientific identity (Amphiprion bicinctus), captive-bred origin, and 'spots/blobs' morphological description with occasional Pearl Eye trait.

additional.name: Reeflings
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (12 cm), tank size (100 L), temperature (24–28°C), pH (8.0–8.4), specific gravity (1.023–1.026), care level (easy/hardy), host anemones, and lifespan (~10 years).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Pomacentridae), wild max length (14 cm TL), depth range (1–30 m), distribution (Red Sea, Gulf of Aden, Chagos Archipelago) and reproductive biology (oviparous, monogamous, sex reversal in 26–145 days).

additional.name: ReefApp
additional.notes: Cross-checked tank size (200 L), salinity (~1.026 SG), reef-safe status, and conspecific aggression (one pair per aquarium).

additional.name: Reef Chasers
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (~10 cm), territorial temperament, hierarchical social structure, and protandrous sex change.

fish.breedingNotes: Captive-bred routinely; pairs spawn on flat substrate near a host; eggs hatch in ~6–11 days; larvae need rotifers then baby brine shrimp in a separate rearing tank.
